meta.title: Tara River Rafting — Rafting Camp Konak | Bosnia & Herzegovina
meta.description: Guided rafting tours on the Tara River, through Europe's deepest canyon. 1 to 4-day trips, riverside bungalows and home-cooked food. Hum, Foča, Bosnia. Season May–October.

meta.ogTitle: Tara River Rafting — Rafting Camp Konak
meta.ogDescription: Guided 1 to 4-day trips through Europe's deepest canyon. Riverside bungalows and a camp where the Tara meets the Piva.

why.p1After: . For years they led guests for other camps, learned every strength and every shortfall — then opened their own: Konak. Everything they picked up on the water and in other camps, they poured into one place.
why.p2Before: No rush, no overbooked slots, no assembly-line tourism. One well-kept camp, a small team, and full attention on
why.p2Strong: the experience on the river and at camp
why.stat1Desc: of guide experience on the Tara
why.stat2Desc: tuned to the last detail, never crowded

camp.body: 55 units, luxury bungalows with private bathrooms, and a spacious auto camp. Home cooking, a welcome drink, and the quiet of the river.

faq.title: Everything you need to know.
faq.q1: Is rafting on the Tara safe?
faq.a1: Yes. You're led by licensed river guides with years of experience, with a mandatory safety briefing before you launch and full protective kit (life jacket, helmet, wetsuit). The Tara has stretches for every level, and we match the route to your group.
faq.q2: Do I need to know how to swim?
faq.a2: You don't need to be a swimmer. Everyone on the water wears a life jacket, and your guide stays with you and calls the shots. Follow the instructions — we handle the rest.
faq.a3: Yes, with a parent or guardian. For younger kids we pick calmer stretches of river. We agree the lower age limit when you book, depending on water levels and the date.
faq.a4: A swimsuit, towel and spare clothes, shoes that can get wet (strappy sandals or old sneakers), sunscreen and good spirits. We provide the wetsuit, life jacket and helmet.
faq.a5: The run with a licensed guide and full kit are always included. Multi-day packages also cover stay and meals (full board). Exact inclusions are listed on each tour page.
faq.a6: Season runs from May to October. Water is highest in spring (more adrenaline); in summer it's calmer and warmer — ideal for families and first-timers.
faq.q7: How long is a one-day rafting trip?
faq.a7: The run itself takes a few hours, with a break for rest and lunch. With kit-up, transfer to the put-in and return to camp, it's a full-day outing.
faq.q8: Is there a difference between weekdays and weekends?
faq.a8: Weekends are usually busier. If you want a quieter slot and fewer boats on the water, we recommend weekdays. Departures are timed around groups and water levels.
faq.a9: We offer luxury bungalows with private bathrooms and a spacious auto camp right on the bank. There's a restaurant with home cooking and shared spaces to hang out.
faq.a10: Yes — dogs are welcome at camp. Please mention it when you book so we can place you in the right stay.
faq.a11: Fastest is our booking page; you can also call, WhatsApp/Viber or email. We'll reply with available dates and a confirmation.
faq.a12: A small deposit usually confirms the booking; the rest is settled when you arrive at camp. We'll confirm details and payment method when you reserve.
faq.a13: Absolutely. Pair rafting with canyoning and mountain day trips (Sutjeska National Park, Durmitor, Zelengora) in one multi-day stay. We'll suggest a plan for the days you have.

meta.title: Camp Konak — Riverside Bungalows, Camper Site & Restaurant | Hum, Foča
meta.description: Rafting Camp Konak on the riverbank in Hum near Foča: 55 units, luxury bungalows with private bathrooms, camper site at €20/night (electric hookup, toilets, dogs welcome) and a home-cooking restaurant.
meta.keywords: rafting camp Konak, luxury bungalows Tara, camper site Bosnia, rafting accommodation Tara, camp on the Drina
meta.ogTitle: Camp Konak — a home on the riverbank
meta.ogDescription: A family-run place at the confluence of the Tara and Piva — luxury bungalows, camper site and home cooking.

hero.lead: Where the Tara and Piva meet to form the Drina, in Hum near Foča. Your base for adventure — warm, peaceful and right on the water.
hero.imageAlt: Rafting Camp Konak on the Tara riverbank

about.naslov: Here you're a guest, not a room number.
about.p1: Camp Konak is a family-run place that has been welcoming adventurers from all over for six years — without a single bad review. It sits right on the bank at the confluence of the Tara and Piva, surrounded by pine forest and the steep walls of the canyon.
about.p2: Everything is built around your comfort: luxury bungalows with private bathrooms, new rafting kit to the highest standards, and professional river guides. No crowds, no rush — just the river, the wilderness and the quiet.
about.p3: After a day on the water the day ends the way only true hosts can manage — riverside barbecue, a welcome rakija (homemade fruit brandy) and the silence of the canyon.
about.imageAlt: Entrance to Camp Konak — stone and timber in front of the mountains

atmosfera.img1Alt: Rafting Camp Konak by the river
atmosfera.img3Alt: Landscape and nature around Camp Konak

stay.naslov: Where you sleep after the river
stay.bungaloviOpis: Comfortable stay with a private bathroom in every unit. Warm, clean and quiet — exactly what you need after a day in the canyon.
stay.autoKampOpis: Spacious pitches for campervans and tents right on the bank. Everything you need in one place, with access to the restaurant.

faq.title: Everything you need to know.
faq.q1: Which tour is best for first-timers?
faq.a1: The one-day trip is perfect for a first taste of the Tara — plenty of excitement without committing to an overnight. If you want the full experience, the three-day package is our most popular.
faq.q2: Do I need to know how to swim?
faq.a2: Not at all. Everyone wears a life jacket and helmet, and the river guide steers the raft. Just don't be afraid of the water and follow the guide's instructions.
faq.a3: Kit, licensed guide, transfer to the put-in, meal(s), insurance and permits. Multi-day packages also include accommodation and full board.
faq.a4: Saturday and Sunday count as weekend for all tours; Friday counts as weekend for multi-day packages only. The price adjusts automatically to the date you pick when booking.
faq.a5: Yes. Children under 6 go free; ages 6–12 pay half price. For younger kids we pick calmer stretches of river.

meta.title: Home-Cooked Balkan Food on the Tara | Camp Konak
meta.description: Camp Konak's kitchen: slow-cooked dishes under the sač (traditional iron bell), farm-raised meat from Zavait, organic vegetables and family-recipe desserts. Breakfast, lunch, dinner and a welcome rakija.
meta.keywords: home cooking rafting camp, sač cooking Tara, traditional Balkan food Bosnia, Camp Konak restaurant, organic food Foča
meta.ogTitle: Food & menu — home-cooked under the sač
meta.ogDescription: A kitchen you'll remember — ingredients from the Spasojević farm and local gardens, sač dishes and a fire-grilled dinner.

hero.lead: Many guests come back for the river — and for what's waiting when they get off the water. We cook from the heart, with ingredients from farms nearby.
hero.imageAlt: Veal under the sač (traditional iron bell) — slow-cooking in the hearth at Camp Konak

intro.lead: Food here is part of the experience, not an afterthought.
intro.p1: In the Konak kitchen everything starts with the ingredient. Meat comes from the Spasojević family farm on the mountain Zavait (1,600 m), vegetables from the gardens nearby, and cheese and dairy from high-altitude pastures. We put on the table what is healthy, organic and raised without rush — the way food used to be. For guests who don't eat meat we prepare vegetarian and vegan dishes; just let us know in advance.
intro.p2: And before anything else — every guest is welcomed with a shot of rakija (homemade fruit brandy). That's simply how we do things here.

brane.p1: Brane has run the camp kitchen since day one — the sač (traditional iron bell used for slow-cooking over embers), the grill and the homemade bread all follow recipes he learned from the Spasojević family. He knows exactly which cut from Zavait goes under the sač, how much patience a good soup needs, and how to make every meal the thing guests talk about when they get home.
brane.note: Home cooking · Spasojević farm · Zavait 1,600 m

menu.item1Opis: The day starts generously — homemade pite (savoury filled pastry) and warm uštipci (fried dough), cured meats and mountain cheeses, omelettes and sausages. Just the fuel you need before a descent down the Tara.
menu.item1Alt: Home-cooked breakfast — eggs, cured ham, cheese and kajmak
menu.item2Opis: A creamy homemade soup to start, then the main event — tender veal from a local calf cooked slowly under the sač (a heavy iron bell placed over hot embers), with homemade bread baked the same way. Patient, over the coals, as it should be done.
menu.item2Alt: Veal under the sač with roast potatoes and soup
menu.item3Naslov: By the fire, with a glass of wine
menu.item3Opis: Grilled meat from local farms with fresh salads from nearby gardens. Everything tastes best by the fire, beside the hearth, with a glass of wine and a conversation that stretches late into the night.
menu.item3Alt: Generous camp dinner — eggs, cured meat and cheese
menu.item4Opis: To finish — homemade sweets made to family recipes passed down through generations. Organic, no artificial additives, exactly as sweet as they should be, and the right note to close the day.
menu.item4Alt: Homemade chocolate cake at lunch at Camp Konak

cta.title: Hungry after the river?
cta.lead: Meals are included in all multi-day packages. For groups and special requests (vegetarian, vegan) let us know in advance.

meta.title: Riverside Bungalows & Camper Site on the Tara | Camp Konak
meta.description: 13 luxury bungalows with private bathrooms and a camper site at the confluence of the Tara and Piva. River quiet, hot water and home cooking — accommodation included with rafting packages. Hum, Foča.
meta.keywords: Tara river bungalows, riverside accommodation Bosnia, luxury bungalows Foča, rafting accommodation, camper site Tara, Camp Konak stay
meta.ogTitle: Accommodation — bungalows & camper site | Camp Konak
meta.ogDescription: 13 luxury bungalows and a camper site on the Tara — private bathrooms, hot water and home cooking.

hero.lead: Our luxury bungalows and camper site sit where the Tara and Piva meet to become the Drina. The quiet of the water, mountain air and home-cooked food a step from your bed.
hero.imageAlt: Camp Konak luxury bungalows on the Tara riverbank

intro.lead: You don't come here to sit in a room.
intro.p1: A day on the Tara empties you out in a way the city never does — the paddling, the water, the sun, the adrenaline. When you come back in the evening, you don't need luxury. You need hot water, a clean bed and quiet. That is exactly what we built.
intro.p2: The camp sits right on the bank, where the Tara and Piva meet and the Drina begins. No traffic, no city noise — just the river working through the night. Guests tell us they've slept better here than anywhere. We've stopped being surprised.

bungalovi.naslov: Thirteen bungalows, each with its own bathroom
bungalovi.p1: The camp has 13 bungalows with 55 beds in total, and every bungalow has its own private bathroom and hot water. That sounds like a small thing — until you've spent a day in the canyon, and then you realise it's everything.
bungalovi.p2: The bungalows are timber, and you feel it. The smell of wood, cool in summer, holding the warmth when the temperature drops. For the extended season we have heaters, so a September evening is just as comfortable.
bungalovi.p3: They're deliberately simple. A clean, comfortable bed, your own bathroom, and that's it. No TV, no fuss, no reason to stay indoors. At Konak your day is on the water, your evening is by the fire with dinner, and the bungalow is there for the one thing you actually need after all that — a quiet sleep to the sound of the river.
bungalovi.p4: If there are two of you booking a bungalow, it's yours alone. We don't fill rooms to capacity.
bungalovi.note: Bungalows come with rafting packages — not available as standalone nights.
bungalovi.imageAlt: Camp Konak luxury bungalow on the riverbank

evening.naslov: The thing you don't plan — and remember most
evening.p1: The best part of the Tara isn't on the river. It happens in the evening, when everyone's back, showered and sitting down at the table. The fire, dinner from Brane's kitchen, a glass of wine, and a conversation that starts about the rapids and ends somewhere else entirely.
evening.p2: People who met in the raft that morning are acting like old friends by nightfall. That's not something we arrange — the river does it for us.
evening.p3: Dogs are welcome. Parking is free and CCTV-monitored. The river is two steps away and asks nothing of you.
evening.imageAlt: Evening at Camp Konak — atmosphere by the river

autoKamp.p1: For those travelling by campervan or tent, our site takes up to 15 vehicles — enough space for everyone to have their own peace, with no neighbours in your window. Each pitch includes sanitation, electric hookup and WiFi, with the home kitchen and the river a few steps away.
autoKamp.p2Text: Simple, fair, and exactly the way camping on the Tara should be.
autoKamp.imageAlt: Camp Konak camper site — riverside pitches

included.i1Opis: Private bathroom with hot water in every bungalow
included.i2Opis: The quiet of the water and a view of where the Tara meets the Piva
included.i3Opis: Connection and electricity when you need them — no overcrowded camp

meta.ogTitle: Corporate Team Building on the Tara River | Camp Konak
meta.ogDescription: A team that shares an adventure on the river bonds stronger on the shore — accommodation, food and activities all in one place.

hero.naslov: Teams that share an adventure grow stronger.
hero.lead: Companies, schools and sports teams — everything you need in one place on the Tara. Rafting, canyoning, accommodation and home-cooked meals without the logistical headache.
hero.imageAlt: Team building group on a rafting trip at Camp Konak

whyKonak.naslov: Everything your team needs — right on the riverbank.
whyKonak.opis: No city transfers, no queuing at a restaurant at 5 p.m. Camp Konak is a self-contained ecosystem: accommodation, food, gear and guides all in one place. Your team spends the day on the water or in the canyon, the evening around the grill with a welcome rakija (local fruit brandy), and the meadow is ready for team games or outdoor presentations.
whyKonak.imageAlt: Group guests in gear outside Camp Konak

activities.naslov: A programme built around your team

items.opis: Running the Tara in the same boat — coordination, trust and adrenaline that bond a team better than any boardroom session.
items.imageAlt: Team rafting on the Tara at Camp Konak

items.opis: Nevidio or Hrčavka — stepping out of the comfort zone together, with experienced guides and top-grade equipment.
items.imageAlt: Group canyoning — descent through the canyon

items.opis: Perućica primeval forest, Lake Trnovačko or Maglić peak — a day in nature as a break from the water and a chance for team connection.
items.imageAlt: Day trip to Sutjeska National Park for teams

items.opis: 13 bungalows — private bathrooms, peaceful riverside setting. Sleeping and dining for groups of up to 60: companies, schools and sports teams.
Full board of home-cooked Bosnian food — dishes from the sač (traditional covered pan), grill and custom menus. Vegetarian and vegan options on request.
A spacious meadow beside the camp — ideal for team games, outdoor presentations, barbecues and evening bonfires.

meta.ogTitle: How to Find Us — Hum, Foča
meta.ogDescription: Camp Konak at the Tara–Piva confluence — about 20 km from Foča, free parking and space for a coach.

hero.lead: Camp Konak sits in Hum, near Foča — right at the confluence of the Tara and Piva rivers, where they join to form the Drina. About 20 km from Foča and just 2 km from the confluence itself.

location.lead: We're on the border of Bosnia & Herzegovina and Montenegro, in the village of Hum near Šćepan Polje. This is the starting point for Tara rafting and an ideal base for day trips to Sutjeska NP, Durmitor and Lake Piva.

info.label: Distance
info.value: ~20 km from Foča · ~2 km from the confluence

info.label: Parking
info.value: Free, CCTV-monitored · space for a coach

routes.naslov: How to get to the camp

items.opis: Via Trnovo and Foča to Hum.
Via Nikšić and Plužine, along the Piva valley to Šćepan Polje and Hum.
Via Trebinje and Nikšić, then along the Piva valley.
Via Užice and Foča to Hum.

items.tekst: Payment at the camp is cash only — we don't accept cards. Foča is the last town on the route with banks and ATMs, so withdraw what you need there. After Foča, there's nowhere to get cash before the camp.
Foča is the last major stop before the canyon. Fuel stations, shops and a pharmacy are all here. Through the canyon to the camp there's nothing — just the river and the cliffs.

The last twenty-odd kilometres run alongside the river through the canyon. The road is narrow and twisty — not one for rushing, but beautiful in its own right. Allow a bit more time than your sat-nav suggests.
Mobile signal can disappear in the canyon. If you think you're lost or running late, call us before you enter the canyon. Download your map offline just in case.
We don't have a set arrival time. Come when it suits you — we'll be here to welcome you.
Free, CCTV-monitored parking, with room for a coach.
Bring a national ID card or passport — the river passes through two countries on part of its course, and the Šćepan Polje border crossing is right next to us.

notesShared.n1: No experience needed — the Tara is ideal even for those sitting in a raft for the very first time.
notesShared.n2: Arrival at camp is flexible — come when it suits you, you're always welcome.
notesShared.n3: Children under 6 go free; ages 6–12 pay half price.
notesShared.n4: Bring an ID card or passport — the river crosses two countries on part of its course.
notesShared.n5: Bring a swimsuit, towel, dry clothes and shoes that can get wet. Everything else we provide.
notesShared.n6: Guests make their own way to camp. For groups of 6 or more we can arrange transfers — charged separately, just get in touch.
notesShared.n7: We offer good discounts for larger groups — give us a call and we'll sort something out.
notesShared.n8: Payment is cash only, at the camp. We do not accept cards.
notesShared.n9: Season: May–October. We reserve the right to assess weather conditions for the safety of our guests.

notIncludedBase.n1: Drinks at the restaurant and on the water

faqShared.q1: Is the tour safe for beginners?
faqShared.a1: Yes. Every raft has a licensed river guide, the kit is new, and everyone gets a safety briefing before launch. The Tara is perfect even for first-time paddlers.
faqShared.a2: Full kit, licensed guide, transfer to the put-in, meal(s) per package, insurance and all fees. Multi-day packages also include accommodation and full board. Payment is cash only.
faqShared.a3: Weekend rates apply on Saturdays and Sundays (and Fridays for multi-day packages). The price adjusts automatically to the date you choose when booking.
faqShared.q4: Can I add Sutjeska or other activities?
faqShared.a4: Yes — alongside your tour you can add Sutjeska National Park, Hrčavka canyoning or horse riding. All on request — just tick them when you book.
faqShared.q5: Someone in our group has dietary requirements — is that a problem?
faqShared.a5: Not at all. Note it in the dietary field when booking (vegetarian, gluten-free, etc.) and we'll adapt the meals.

notesCanyon.n1: No minimum group size — we guide couples and solo travellers too. You can join an existing group.
notesCanyon.n2: Age: suitable for most ages; children must be accompanied by an adult.
notesCanyon.n3: No prior experience needed — training is included before you enter the canyon.
notesCanyon.n4: Payment: deposit on booking, balance at camp (cash or card).
notesCanyon.n5: Documents: ID card or passport required for all participants.
notesCanyon.n6: We reserve the right to assess weather conditions and adjust the programme for safety.

jednodnevni.title: One-Day Rafting
jednodnevni.subtitle: Eighteen kilometres and eighteen rapids through the finest part of the Tara canyon. No overnight, no complications — one day you'll remember for years.
jednodnevni.intro: This is the descent everyone asks for. Eighteen kilometres from Brštanovica to Hum, through the most thrilling part of the canyon — the rapids come one after another, and between them the river calms and lets you look up at walls that rise a thousand metres. No experience needed: in the raft is a river guide who has run the Tara for over twenty years and knows every rapid by name. You paddle when he says, and enjoy the rest of the time.
jednodnevni.p1Text: We welcome you at camp in Hum, where the Tara meets the Piva. A welcome rakija (homemade brandy) and a quick rundown of the day. No fixed time — come when it suits you.
jednodnevni.p2Text: Pick your kit: wetsuit, boots, life jacket, helmet and paddle. Your guide runs the briefing — how to paddle, how to sit, what to do if someone falls in. Short, clear, and only when everyone's ready — then we go.
jednodnevni.p3Text: We drive you to the put-in in our vehicles. The ride along the canyon rim is part of the experience — the view of the Tara from above sets the scene for what's coming.
jednodnevni.p4Text: The most spectacular stretch of the Tara. We stop for a swim in emerald water and for photos at spots no road can reach. In May the current is fast and the descent takes about an hour and a half; in August it's calmer and warmer, stretching to three and a half hours.
jednodnevni.p5Text: Rafts pull in at camp. Hot water, shower, dry clothes — and then, if you booked with lunch, the reason so many guests come back.
jednodnevni.menu1Text: A creamy homemade soup to start. Then veal cooked under the sač (a traditional iron bell) — from local calves, not from something that spent two months travelling from Argentina — with homemade bread baked the same way, over embers. Fresh salads from gardens nearby, and kajmak and cheese from the Spasojević family farm at Zavait, 1,600 metres up. Home-baked desserts to finish.
jednodnevni.menu2Text: The sač at our place is Brane's domain — a cook since he was nineteen, trained in what were once the finest kitchens of Yugoslavia. What he lifts out from under the embers is not something you learn in a hurry.
jednodnevni.menuNote: Lunch is optional — but it's the reason so many guests come back.
jednodnevni.i1: Full rafting kit (wetsuit, boots, life jacket, helmet, paddle)
jednodnevni.i2: Licensed river guide in every raft
jednodnevni.i4: Transfer to the put-in and back in our vehicles
jednodnevni.notesExtra1: No minimum group size — we guide couples and solo travellers too. You can join an existing group.

jednodnevni.similar2Opis: The package guests choose most — and keep coming back to.

dvodnevni.title: Two-Day Package
dvodnevni.subtitle: An evening at camp and a full day on the river. For those who don't want the adventure to rush past — but to step into it slowly, with dinner by the fire.
dvodnevni.intro: You arrive without rushing, settle into a lux bungalow, and spend the first evening exactly as the Tara demands — with a welcome rakija (homemade brandy), a home-cooked dinner and the sound of the river putting you to sleep. The next morning, rested and fed, you get in the raft and run eighteen kilometres down the finest part of the canyon.
dvodnevni.p1Text: We greet you with a welcome rakija and settle you into a lux bungalow with a private bathroom. The rest of the day is yours — the river is a step away, the bank is your own, no rush. In the evening, dinner from our kitchen, by the fire, with a conversation that stretches late into the night.
dvodnevni.p2Text: Breakfast at camp — burek, fried dough rolls, kajmak and Spasojević cheese. Then kit up, briefing and a minibus ride to Brštanovica. Next: the descent — eighteen kilometres down the Tara and into the Drina, eighteen rapids, swims in emerald water.
dvodnevni.p3Text: Back at camp, hot water and lunch — veal under the sač, soup, homemade bread. After lunch the package ends, but nobody's pushing you out — stay as long as you like.
dvodnevni.menu1Text: Grilled meat from local farms, fresh salads from nearby gardens. By the fire, with a glass of wine.
dvodnevni.menu2Text: Homemade burek and warm fried dough rolls, cured meats, kajmak and Spasojević cheese from Zavait, omelettes and sausages.
dvodnevni.menu3Text: Creamy homemade soup, veal cooked under the sač from local calves, bread under the sač, salads. Home-baked desserts.
dvodnevni.menu4Text: All from Brane's hands — a cook since nineteen, trained in what were once the finest kitchens of Yugoslavia.
dvodnevni.i1: 1 night in a lux bungalow with private bathroom
dvodnevni.i4: Licensed river guide and safety briefing
dvodnevni.i5: Transfer to the put-in and back
dvodnevni.similar1Opis: 18 km and 18 rapids — one day to remember.
dvodnevni.similar2Opis: The package guests choose most — and keep coming back to.
dvodnevni.similar3Opis: 76 km — the full run, caves, waterfalls and a night in the canyon.

dvodnevni.metaDescription: Two-day rafting package: 1 night in a lux bungalow, 3 meals and an 18 km descent. Weekday from €100, weekend €120.

trodnevni.title: Three-Day Package
trodnevni.subtitle: The package guests choose most — and the reason they come back. Rafting, two evenings at camp, and one more day to see what the river can't show you.
trodnevni.intro: Three days is just right. The first evening you settle in and get to know the camp. The second day is the river — eighteen kilometres, eighteen rapids. And the third day, pleasantly tired, you choose how to spend it: an activity in Sutjeska National Park, or simply another quiet morning by the river before heading home.
trodnevni.p1Text: Welcome with rakija (homemade brandy) and check-in to a lux bungalow. An evening at camp — dinner from our kitchen, the fire, the river, conversation. No schedule, no rush.
trodnevni.p2Text: Breakfast, then kit up and briefing. A minibus to Brštanovica, and then the descent — eighteen kilometres down the Tara and into the Drina, through the most thrilling part of the canyon. Stops for swimming and photos. Back at camp, hot water and lunch under the sač. In the evening a second dinner, and the kind of conversation that stretches until the fire burns down.
trodnevni.p3Text: After breakfast you choose: a full-day activity in Sutjeska National Park, or a quiet morning by the river before you leave. The third-day activity is arranged on request — guests who've already booked a rafting package get our best rate.
trodnevni.menu1Text: Grilled meat from local farms, fresh salads from nearby gardens, by the fire with a glass of wine.
trodnevni.menu2Text: Homemade burek and fried dough rolls, cured meats, kajmak and Spasojević cheese from 1,600 metres up.
trodnevni.menu3Text: Creamy soup, veal cooked under the sač from local calves, bread under the sač, salads, home-baked desserts.
trodnevni.menu4Text: Another evening by the fire — grilled meat, salads, good company.
trodnevni.menu5Text: A Balkan breakfast before the road home.
trodnevni.menu6Text: All made by Brane — a cook with forty years behind him, trained in what were once the finest kitchens of Yugoslavia.
trodnevni.i1: 2 nights in a lux bungalow with private bathroom
trodnevni.i4: Licensed river guide and safety briefing
trodnevni.i5: Transfer to the put-in and back

cijela-tara.title: The Full Tara — 4 Days
cijela-tara.subtitle: The entire navigable length of the Tara — seventy-six kilometres, from Đurđevića bridge to Hum. Caves, waterfalls, and one night at the deepest point of the canyon, far from everything.
cijela-tara.intro: This is an expedition, not a day trip. We cover the entire navigable stretch of the Tara, through Europe's deepest canyon — one thousand three hundred metres of rock above your head. We drive to the put-in via the Durmitor Ring Road, one of the most beautiful roads in the Balkans and worth the journey in itself. And one night we sleep where the canyon is deepest, in a small inn by the river — far from any road, far from signal, under the stars.
cijela-tara.introExtra: This stretch of the Tara hosted the 2009 Rafting World Championship.
cijela-tara.p1Text: Welcome with rakija (homemade brandy), check-in to a lux bungalow, an evening at camp with a home-cooked dinner. Getting ready for what lies ahead.
cijela-tara.p2Title: The Durmitor Ring and the start of the expedition
cijela-tara.p2Text: After breakfast we set off on the Durmitor Ring Road — a route that climbs over the mountain and opens views you don't forget. We reach the point where the Tara becomes navigable and launch the rafts. First day on the water: caves, waterfalls, untouched wilderness. We sleep at the inn at the deepest point of the canyon.
cijela-tara.p3Title: Through the heart of the canyon
cijela-tara.p3Text: We continue downstream through the deepest part of the canyon. The river changes face from hour to hour — calm pools, then rapids, then waterfalls dropping straight into the water. Back at camp, dinner and rest.
cijela-tara.p4Text: Breakfast at camp and a quiet morning before the drive home.
cijela-tara.canyonStay: We spend the night in the canyon at a small inn at the deepest point of the Tara — clean, well-kept and full of character. Meals are buffet style. This is a place you can almost only reach by river.
cijela-tara.menu1Text: Breakfasts and dinners at camp — homemade burek, fried dough rolls, Spasojević kajmak, grilled meat from local farms, veal under the sač from Brane's kitchen. In the canyon, at the inn, meals are buffet style. Every meal is included in the price.
cijela-tara.i1: 3 nights (lux bungalow + 1 night at the canyon inn)
cijela-tara.i4: Licensed river guide and safety briefing
cijela-tara.i5: All transfers (including the Durmitor Ring Road)

nevidio.title: Nevidio Canyoning
nevidio.subtitle: The most demanding canyon in Montenegro — the Komarnica river beneath Durmitor. Jumps, descents and swims through tight passages, for true adventurers.
nevidio.intro: The finest and most demanding canyon in the area — jumps, water descents and swims through the tight passages of the Komarnica river beneath Durmitor. A full-day guided trip from camp with experienced guides, full kit and transfers. Season Jun–Oct.
nevidio.p1Text: We welcome you at camp and fit you with full kit: wetsuit, life jacket, helmet and carabiners. Before departure everyone goes through a short safety briefing with the licensed guide.
nevidio.p2Text: We drive you to Nevidio canyon in Montenegro, beneath the Durmitor massif. The mountain drive sets the mood for the adventure ahead.
nevidio.p3Text: With your guide you enter the canyon and work through its narrow sections — jumps, natural slides and swims in emerald water. The pace is set to the group, but the canyon demands good fitness.
nevidio.p4Text: After exiting the canyon there's lunch and a rest before the drive back to Camp Konak. The evening is yours by the river, with memories of the most demanding canyon in the area.
nevidio.faq_a1: Nevidio is the most demanding canyon in the area and is intended for more experienced adventurers. For a first canyoning experience we recommend Hrčavka — a gentler canyon in Sutjeska National Park.
nevidio.faq_a2: Full kit, licensed guide, transfer to the canyon, lunch and insurance. Everything is organised from camp — no hidden costs.
nevidio.faq_a3: Weekend rates apply on Saturdays and Sundays. The price adjusts automatically to the date you choose when booking.
nevidio.faq_q4: Do I need to know how to swim?
nevidio.faq_a4: Swimming ability is not strictly required, but a basic ability to swim and good physical fitness are recommended given the canyon's difficulty and the length of the trip.
nevidio.faq_q5: When is the Nevidio canyoning season?
nevidio.faq_a5: We run Nevidio canyoning from June to October, when conditions in the canyon are most favourable and safe for groups.
nevidio.metaTitle: Nevidio Canyoning — Komarnica Canyon, Durmitor | Camp Konak
nevidio.metaDescription: Nevidio canyoning (Komarnica canyon, Durmitor): the most demanding canyon in Montenegro. Jumps, descents and swims with a licensed guide. Weekday from €130, weekend €140.

hrcavka.title: Hrčavka Canyoning
hrcavka.subtitle: A gentler canyon in Sutjeska National Park near Tjentište — perfect for a first canyoning experience. Jumps into pools and natural water slides.
hrcavka.intro: A gentler canyon close to camp, ideal for a first canyoning experience. In Sutjeska National Park near Tjentište, emerald pools and natural water slides await with an experienced guide. Season Jun–Oct.
hrcavka.p1Text: We welcome you at camp and get you into your kit: wetsuit, life jacket, helmet and carabiners. The guide explains movement technique through the canyon and the safety rules.
hrcavka.p2Text: A short drive takes us to Hrčavka canyon near Tjentište, in the heart of Sutjeska National Park. On the way you soak up the mountain scenery before entering the canyon.
hrcavka.p3Text: With your guide you work through the canyon — jumps into emerald pools and natural slides. The pace is suited to the group; it's ideal even for those entering a canyon for the very first time.
hrcavka.p4Text: After the adventure we return to Camp Konak. The rest of the day is yours — relax by the river or combine with other activities.
hrcavka.faq_q1: Is Hrčavka good for a first canyoning trip?
hrcavka.faq_a1: Yes — Hrčavka is a gentler canyon in Sutjeska National Park and one of the best choices for a first canyoning experience. The jumps and slides are suitable even for beginners.
hrcavka.faq_a2: Full kit, licensed guide, transfer to the canyon and insurance. Meals are not included — you can add them with a camp stay.
hrcavka.faq_a3: Weekend rates apply on Saturdays and Sundays. The price adjusts automatically to the date you choose when booking.
hrcavka.faq_q4: Do I need to know how to swim?
hrcavka.faq_a4: Not essential. Everyone wears a life jacket and helmet, and the guide leads the group through the canyon. Just don't be afraid of the water and follow the instructions.
hrcavka.faq_q5: When is the Hrčavka canyoning season?
hrcavka.faq_a5: We run Hrčavka canyoning from June to October, when water levels in the canyon are ideal for a safe and enjoyable descent.
hrcavka.metaTitle: Hrčavka Canyoning — Sutjeska National Park | Camp Konak
hrcavka.metaDescription: Hrčavka canyoning in Sutjeska NP (Tjentište): a beginner-friendly canyon with jumps into pools and natural slides. Weekday from €120, weekend €130.

kada-na-taru.title: When Is the Best Time to Raft the Tara?
kada-na-taru.excerpt: May or August? What the river is like month by month — when it's fastest, when it's warmest, and who should choose which.
kada-na-taru.metaTitle: Best Time to Raft the Tara River — Month by Month | Camp Konak
kada-na-taru.metaDescription: May or August? A guide to the Tara rafting season — water levels by month, when it's fastest, when it's warmest, and which month suits whom. From guides with 20+ years on the river.
kada-na-taru.lead: The question we get most often before a booking. There isn't one answer — because the Tara in May is not the Tara in August. Here's how the river looks month by month, so you can pick the Tara that fits you.
kada-na-taru.imageAlt: Rafting the Tara — when is the best time for a run

p: If you want adrenaline and fast water — May and June. If you're bringing a family and want warm water and a calmer run — July and August. If you want quiet, no crowds and the canyon's finest colours — September. Our season runs from May to October.

blocks.h: May and June — the Tara shows its teeth
p: This is when snowmelt from Durmitor and Sinjajevina pours into the Tara. The river is fast, full, and the rapids are at their fiercest. A run that takes three and a half hours in August flies by in an hour and a half in May — the river simply carries you.
The water is cold. But we kit you out in a wetsuit, boots and a life vest, so it doesn't feel the way it sounds. What you do feel is the river doing its job while you hold the paddle.
This is the month for people who want a real ride. Not for those looking for a gentle afternoon on the water.

blocks.h: July and August — the Tara for everyone
p: Water levels drop, the river warms up, and it grows gentler. All eighteen rapids are still there — but they're softer, and between them there's far more time to look up at the canyon walls.

This is when we swim the most. We stop at emerald pools, jump from the rocks, bask on the raft. The run lasts longer — three and a half hours — and that isn't a drawback; it's exactly why people come then.
If you're bringing children or someone sitting in a raft for the first time, this is your month.

blocks.h: September and October — the canyon to yourself
p: The crowds leave; the river stays. The water is still pleasantly warm, the days are clear, and the canyon takes on colours you don't see in summer — beech turns gold, the rock reddens under the lower sun.
Fewer people on the water means everything runs without rush. For us these are the finest days on the Tara, even if guests rarely know that in advance.

The Tara is drinkable — and that's not marketing. It's a river you can scoop up in your palm and drink, on any stretch. The first year people are surprised; the next year they tell the story themselves.
Whichever month you pick, there's a guide in the boat who's been running the Tara for over twenty years. They know the line for that water level, where to stop and where not to. You just show up.

sta-ponijeti-na-rafting.title: What to Pack for Rafting the Tara
sta-ponijeti-na-rafting.excerpt: A proven checklist — what we provide and what you bring yourself: clothing, footwear, sun protection and the small things that make a difference on the river.
sta-ponijeti-na-rafting.metaTitle: What to Pack for Rafting the Tara — Checklist | Camp Konak
sta-ponijeti-na-rafting.metaDescription: What to pack for rafting the Tara: clothing, footwear, sun protection and a waterproof bag. A practical guide from Camp Konak — what we supply and what you bring.
sta-ponijeti-na-rafting.lead: Rafting the Tara doesn't take much kit — most of it comes from us. But a few small things can turn a good day on the river into a perfect one. Here's a proven list, from guides with over 20 years on the Tara.
sta-ponijeti-na-rafting.imageAlt: What to pack for rafting — gear on the Tara

blocks.h: What we provide (you don't need to bring)
p: Full rafting kit on site: wetsuit, neoprene boots, wind jacket, life vest, helmet and paddle. Everything is fitted to your size before we leave.
The raft also carries waterproof barrels for anything you want to keep dry on the run. Every trip is led by a licensed guide — your safety and navigation through the canyon are on them.
Swimwear or shorts (worn under the wetsuit), a towel and dry clothes for after the run. Footwear that can get wet — sandals, trainers or boots you don't mind soaking.
Sun protection: cream, a cap or hat, glasses with a strap. The sun on the water and in the canyon is stronger than you expect, even under cloud.
Toiletries, a warm layer for evenings at camp, a head torch and a phone charger. You sleep in a bungalow, but nights by the river can be cooler.
A waterproof pouch or dry bag for documents and your phone on the water, plus a little cash for anything you need at camp.
Jewellery, watches and valuables — leave them in your room or at home. Large amounts of cash aren't needed.
A phone without waterproof protection — always keep it in a sealed case or dry bag. There's no room for risk on the water.
Swimwear, towel, dry clothes, sun protection and water shoes. For multi-day trips — warm layers and toiletries.
Leave the kit, vest, helmet, guide and meals to us — that's our job.

np-sutjeska-vodic.title: Sutjeska National Park — A Day Trip from Camp
np-sutjeska-vodic.excerpt: A full-day trip: Perućica primeval forest, Lake Trnovačko and Maglić. What you can fit into one day and how we organise it.
np-sutjeska-vodic.metaTitle: Sutjeska National Park — Day Trip from Camp Konak | Perućica, Trnovačko, Maglić
np-sutjeska-vodic.metaDescription: A full-day trip to Sutjeska National Park from Camp Konak: Perućica primeval forest, Lake Trnovačko and Maglić. What fits in one day and how we arrange it.
np-sutjeska-vodic.lead: While you're here, Bosnia and Herzegovina's oldest national park is an hour's drive from camp. For guests staying several days, a day in Sutjeska is a natural break from the river — and many say it was the highlight of their stay.
np-sutjeska-vodic.imageAlt: Sutjeska National Park — a day trip from camp

p: One of the last primeval forests in Europe. Never felled, never touched — trees that grow, fall and rot exactly as they did a thousand years ago. You enter only with a park guide, and that's a good thing: without one you'd walk past everything and see nothing.
From the Dragoš Sedlo viewpoint the whole forest opens up, and Skakavac waterfall drops some eighty metres into the valley.

A heart-shaped glacial lake at 1,517 metres, ringed by Volujak and Maglić. You hike up to it — not a stroll, but not an expedition either. The water is painfully cold and clear to the bottom. It's one of those places people come back to these mountains for.
At 2,386 metres, the highest peak in Bosnia and Herzegovina. For those in good shape with a full day to spare. On a clear day you can see Durmitor and half of Montenegro from the top.
We organise Sutjeska trips for guests on a multi-day package with us. We tailor it to how many of you there are, how fit you are and how much time you have — then choose Perućica, Trnovačko, or both.
Price on request. Guests who've already booked a rafting package get our best rate.
Decent trainers or hiking boots, a jacket (it's colder up there than in the canyon, even in summer), water and something to eat. Everything else we sort together.

aktivnosti-na-tari.title: What to Do on the Tara Besides Rafting
aktivnosti-na-tari.excerpt: Nevidio and Hrčavka canyoning, horseback riding above Europe's deepest canyon, Sutjeska NP, Durmitor — what you can layer onto a rafting stay.
aktivnosti-na-tari.metaTitle: What to Do on the Tara Besides Rafting | Camp Konak
aktivnosti-na-tari.metaDescription: Nevidio and Hrčavka canyoning, horseback riding above Europe's deepest canyon, Sutjeska NP, Durmitor — everything you can combine with rafting at Camp Konak.
aktivnosti-na-tari.lead: Rafting is why most people come. But when you stay a few days, the river turns out to be only the beginning — everything around it is worth just as much.
aktivnosti-na-tari.imageAlt: Activities and day trips around the Tara

p: Two canyons, two completely different days.

Hrčavka sits in Sutjeska National Park near Tjentište — gentler, pure enjoyment. Waterfalls, deep pools, natural slides. If this is your first canyon, start here. Fit teenagers from fourteen up can join.

Nevidio is another story. The Komarnica canyon under Durmitor — the last canyon conquered in Europe. Narrow passages, jumps of up to eight metres, swimming through ice-cold water. This one is for fit adventurers, and you don't enter without a licensed guide.

blocks.h: Horseback riding above Europe's deepest canyon
p: This is the activity guests don't expect — which is why it surprises them most. You ride up on the rim of the Tara's deepest canyon, with 1,300 metres of gorge beneath you. Looking down on the river you ran yesterday is something else entirely.
We arrange it on request — get in touch and we'll sort the details.
Perućica primeval forest, Lake Trnovačko, Maglić. A full-day trip from camp for guests on a multi-day package. More detail in a separate article.

The Durmitor Ring is one of the most beautiful roads in the Balkans — and we drive it every year on the way to the four-day tour put-in. Lake Piva is on the route: turquoise, quiet, water that looks painted.

Easiest through a multi-day package. Rafting is the spine; everything else grafts on — a day on the water, a day in the canyon, a day in the mountains. Tell us how many days you have and what pulls you, and we'll build it.

meta.title: Day Trips & Combinations — Rafting + Sutjeska, Durmitor, Canyoning | Konak
meta.description: Trips and combinations from Camp Konak: rafting plus Sutjeska NP, canyoning or Durmitor. Combine multiple adventures in one stay. Mountain day trips (Perućica, Zelengora, Trnovačko) on request.
meta.keywords: day trips Tara, rafting combinations, rafting Sutjeska, rafting Durmitor, excursions Foča, combined rafting packages
meta.ogTitle: Day Trips & Combinations — Rafting + Sutjeska, Durmitor, Canyoning

items.imageAlt: Rafting and a day trip to Sutjeska National Park

items.opis: A double dose of adrenaline — running the Tara and pushing through Nevidio or Hrčavka. For those who can't stop.
Adrenaline on the river, then a relaxed day on Durmitor by Black Lake. Price on request.

items.opis: Hiking up to the heart-shaped lake below Maglić peak — add it to your rafting stay.
Alpine lakes and sweeping Zelengora scenery — we arrange it from the camp.
A short detour to Lake Piva — a peaceful break by the water.
Riding along the rim of the Tara's deepest canyon — looking down at the river 1,300 metres below. Arranged on request.
items.imageAlt: View of the Tara canyon — horse riding at the viewpoint

cta.headline: Let us build your itinerary
cta.lead: Tell us how many days you're staying and what interests you — we'll put together a tailor-made package.

meta.title: Equipment & Safety — Licensed Guides, New Gear | Camp Konak
meta.description: How we look after your safety on the Tara: certified guides, up-to-date equipment meeting modern standards, a safety briefing and weather assessment before every run.
meta.keywords: rafting safety, rafting gear, certified river guides, rafting safety briefing, is rafting safe, helmets life vests rafting
meta.ogDescription: Certified guides, new gear and clear rules on every trip.

hero.lead: The river commands respect, not contempt. That's why nothing is left to chance here — from equipment to the weather check.
hero.imageAlt: Certified river guide and group in gear on the Tara

intro.subheading: Adventure and safety aren't opposites — here they go hand in hand.
intro.p1: With over 20 years on the Tara, our guides know the river in every mood — glassy calm and in full flood. That kind of experience can't be replaced by any certificate. But the certificates are there too, along with proven gear and clear rules we follow on every trip, no exceptions.

guides.naslov: A certified guide in every boat
guides.lead: Every trip is led by a certified guide who knows the river by heart — every rapid, every current and every safe spot to rest. They choose the line, call the commands and adjust the pace to the group. You just listen and enjoy.

equipment.naslov: New equipment, up to modern standards
equipment.p1: Gear is the one area where we never cut corners. Our equipment is new and meets current standards — boats, life vests, helmets, neoprene suits, paddles. None of it is inherited, borrowed or 'still good enough'.
equipment.p2: We invest in gear continuously, every season. It's a significant cost, and it's where many camps make savings — we don't. Simply put: the person sitting in your boat is someone's father, someone's child, and that's the only calculation that matters to us.
equipment.p3: Gear is regularly maintained and inspected. Before each season and throughout it, we check everything — every vest, every helmet, every boat. Anything showing the slightest sign of wear does not go back on the water.

items.opis: Keeps you warm even in cold water
Protect your feet on the rocks
Buoyancy and safety in the water
Required on every run
Light and durable

briefing.lead: Before every trip the guide explains the paddle commands, how to behave in rapids and what to do if someone goes overboard. Brief, clear and never skipped — we don't push off until everyone is ready. Not one trip has ever launched without a briefing, and none ever will.

weather.lead: The river and weather can change, and guest safety is always our first priority. We reserve the right to postpone or adjust a trip if conditions aren't right. We'd rather disappoint you on one day than take risks — and not a single guest has ever held it against us.

noExp.lead: Basic fitness and a sense of adventure are all you need — you'll learn everything else on the spot, with the guide in the boat beside you the whole time.

meta.title: FAQ — Tara Rafting Questions Answered | Camp Konak
meta.description: Answers to the most common questions about rafting on the Tara: safety, experience level, duration, minimum group size, children, documents and season. Everything you need to know before arriving at Camp Konak.
meta.keywords: Tara rafting FAQ, is rafting safe, rafting with children, rafting documents, how long does rafting take, rafting season Tara
meta.ogDescription: Straight answers — the questions guests ask most before heading to the Tara.

hero.lead: Straight and honest — the questions guests ask most before coming to the Tara. If you can't find what you're looking for, just get in touch.

items.odgovor: Yes. All trips are led by certified guides, you receive complete and well-maintained equipment, and before every departure we run a safety briefing — covering paddle commands, how to navigate rapids and what to do in any situation.
No. Basic fitness and a sense of adventure are all you need. You'll learn everything on the spot, with the guide in the boat beside you the entire time.

It depends on water levels and time of season — roughly 1.5 hours in May when the water is fastest, up to around 3.5 hours in August. That's the run itself, not including the briefing, transport and any meal.

items.pitanje: Is there a minimum group size?
items.odgovor: No minimum — we guide solo travellers and couples too. You're welcome to join an existing group.
Yes, rafting is a great family adventure. Children under 6 are free; ages 6–12 pay half price. For the youngest guests we choose calmer stretches and adjust the pace.

items.pitanje: Do I need ID for rafting?
items.odgovor: Yes. The river passes through two countries on part of its course, so a national ID card or passport is required.
The season runs from May to October. Water levels are highest in spring (stronger adrenaline), while summer offers calmer, warmer water — ideal for families and beginners.

cta.headline: Still have a question?
cta.lead: Write to us or give us a call — we're quick to reply and happy to help with anything.
